inlineContent.text: If you want to apply a theme everywhere in your app, or on specific view, one special
. This object will get a theme you instanciated and apply it as environment value. It will also instanciates some environment objects the components may need.

tasks.title: Navigations and themeable view, a step further

inlineContent.text: Yes! One view to rule them all!
Nope! You must ust the dedicated OUDS view to inject in subviews the theme
Which thing defines the theme to use everywhere?
Of course you can mix views from SwiftUI and OUDS
Feel free to mix components! If you have some bugs, submit us issues on GitHub ❤️
Can you mix SwiftUI and OUDS components?
OUDS iOS Swift Package provide some components with rendering depending to the OS (e.g. tab bars)
Liquid Glass is applied on navigation elements like tab bar
No, Liquid Glass can be applied
Components have all the same rendering whatever is the OS version

abstract.text: Discovering a new framework can be exciting and scary.
With this tutorial, you will learn how to embed OUDS Swift Package and define views tailored for your brand.
It has beend esigned for local events and meetups.
Let’s play!

tutorial-onboarding-3-1.png.alt: A simple view with some component inside

doc://OUDSThemesContract/tutorials/Table.title: Import of the Swift Package and choose its librairies

abstract.text: Let’s suppose we want to define a view for corporate apps with headings, texts, buttons and images.
How can we do that?
Since you have learned how OUDS works with theme, themeable view, components, tuning and view modifiers, it is time now to discover some online resources.
Let’s suppose we want to define a view for corporate apps with headings, texts, buttons and images.
How can we do that?
OUDS iOS is exposed through an open source Swift Package hosted on GitHub, and provides several librairies for your needs.
These librairies help to split products and make users able to select only what they need.
SwiftUI is the only official supported framework for Apple platforms.
Now you are ready to use more components than a simple button!
